note: Public signals are summarized from cited public sources. The agentic CMMS/EAM implications and fit scores are the author's analysis, not claims that the companies run this exact MAS architecture.
score_scale: 1 = low relevance, 5 = very strong relevance for the dimension

examples.public_signal: MBT described its Chongqing site as a full-process AI-empowered chiller lighthouse factory; Midea Industrial Technology also presents a large smart-factory footprint.
examples.agentic_eam_implication: Energy-aware chiller maintenance, compressor reliability, spare-parts forecasting, and after-sales service can be connected into one review package.

examples.sector: Heavy equipment, construction machinery, intelligent factories
examples.public_signal: SANY describes No. 18 Factory as a 100,000-square-meter intelligent manufacturing pilot factory approved by China's Ministry of Industry and Information Technology.
examples.agentic_eam_implication: Factory maintenance, machine health, field-service telemetry, warranty, and MRO parts can be orchestrated by specialized agents.

examples.public_signal: State Grid examples show drone inspection, AI defect recognition, work-order dispatch, and data-set construction for transmission-line defect recognition.
examples.agentic_eam_implication: Inspection findings can become policy-gated work packages that coordinate asset criticality, weather risk, crew safety, and approval routing.

examples.sector: Rail transit equipment and lifecycle service
examples.public_signal: CRRC describes SmartCare intelligent O&M as integrating vehicle and ground data to provide lifecycle intelligent operation and maintenance services.
examples.agentic_eam_implication: Rail fleet maintenance is ideal for safety-critical, explainable, human-approved multi-agent decisions across vehicles, depots, parts, and service contracts.

examples.sector: Industrial digital infrastructure and smart-factory connectivity
examples.public_signal: Huawei's fully connected factory materials emphasize IT/OT convergence, Wi-Fi 7/UWB, visualized OT networks, and equipment data transmission.
examples.agentic_eam_implication: Agentic CMMS/EAM depends on reliable, governed data flow from machines, networks, edge systems, and enterprise platforms.

examples.sector: Oil, gas, refining, petrochemical process operations
examples.public_signal: Sinopec describes smart-factory construction on the Sinopec Smart Cloud industrial Internet platform and closed-loop production optimization.
examples.agentic_eam_implication: Process plants need policy-aware maintenance, risk-ranked inspection, turnaround planning, permit/LOTO checks, and spare-parts co-optimization.

examples.sector: Industrial Internet platform and mass customization
examples.public_signal: Haier describes COSMOPlat as an industrial Internet platform covering industrial platform construction, intelligent factory services, software/hardware integration, and energy management.
examples.agentic_eam_implication: A platform that connects factories, users, energy, and ecosystem partners can provide the shared context layer for cross-factory maintenance agents.

examples.sector: Steel manufacturing and heavy process assets
examples.public_signal: Baosteel presents BaoVision online surface inspection technology for high-speed production, and public reports describe Baowu's broader digital and intelligent transformation.
examples.agentic_eam_implication: High-speed process inspection and heavy-asset maintenance can be linked to defect triage, root-cause analysis, reliability improvement, and quality-cost trade-offs.
